docs/reviews/RFC-044-native-mobile-app-performance-review.md:10:- **Section**: RFC-046 Technical Design §3.1 / `apps/tma/src/app/api/pusher/auth/route.ts`
docs/reviews/RFC-044-native-mobile-app-performance-review.md:11:- **Issue**: RFC-046 (Phase 3) proposes a `private-user-${userId}` Pusher channel for real-time subscription status invalidation. However, the existing `/api/pusher/auth` handler explicitly rejects any channel not starting with `presence-chat-` (line 31). This will cause auth failures for all monetization state updates.
docs/reviews/RFC-044-native-mobile-app-performance-review.md:12:- **Suggestion**: Update `/api/pusher/auth/route.ts` to allow `private-user-` channels, verifying that the `userId` in the channel name matches the authenticated `user.id` to prevent cross-user state snooping.
docs/reviews/RFC-044-native-mobile-app-performance-review.md:16:- **Issue**: RFC-046 implements tiered access (10 swaps per 30 days limit for expired/trial users) enforced via `MonetizationPolicyService`. It relies exclusively on Telegram Stars for payments. RFC-044 (Mobile MVP) defers Apple/Google IAP. This creates a deadlock: users signing in via Apple/Google (mobile-only) will hit the 10-swap limit and have **zero path to subscribe**, effectively bricking the app for high-engagement mobile-only users.
docs/reviews/RFC-044-native-mobile-app-performance-review.md:22:- **Suggestion**: Explicitly include `pusher-js` or `pusher-websocket-react-native` in M3. Leverage the `packages/shared` extraction to share Pusher event handlers (e.g., `new-message`, `subscription:status_changed`) between TMA and Mobile.

docs/solutions/logic-errors/client-side-swap-gate-blocks-limited-users-before-server-quota-check.md:12:Expired trial/subscription users see a generic (no-variant) subscription modal when attempting to send a swap request, even though RFC-046 grants them a swap quota (10 per rolling 30-day window, admin-configurable).
docs/solutions/logic-errors/client-side-swap-gate-blocks-limited-users-before-server-quota-check.md:16:`useSwapOfferGate` (pre-RFC-046 code) checks `subscriptionStatus` client-side and blocks `Expired`/`None` users before the swap API call is ever made:

docs/solutions/logic-errors/client-side-swap-gate-blocks-limited-users-before-server-quota-check.md:48:When migrating from binary access (allowed/blocked) to tiered access (full/limited/renewal_failed), audit every client-side gate that checks subscription status. docs/rfcs/RFC-044-native-mobile-app.md:28:- Premium/subscription features in mobile MVP. **RFC-046 interaction**: The `MonetizationPolicyService` (RFC-046) enforces tiered access on shared endpoints (swap initiation, chat creation). Mobile-only users cannot purchase Telegram Stars subscriptions and will operate at the LIMITED access level (10 swap requests/month, restricted chat initiation) until App Store/Google Play billing is implemented. This is accepted for MVP — the LIMITED tier provides functional access. Future: add native IAP billing in a post-launch milestone to unlock PAID tier for mobile-only users.

docs/rfcs/RFC-044-native-mobile-app.md:847:2. **Channel scope**: RFC-046 added `private-user-*` channels for subscription status events (`pusher.ts:57-64`). The auth route rejects non-`presence-chat-` channels at line 35. Extend to authorize `private-user-{userId}` channels (verify `userId` matches authenticated user).
docs/rfcs/RFC-044-native-mobile-app.md:1325:  > **R8 — Why the split**: The previous version described a full offline write queue with ordering guarantees alongside "no offline queue in V1" — these were mutually exclusive. Research into what production apps actually ship (Stripe, Airbnb, Uber) confirms: V1 uses exponential backoff + idempotency keys with manual retry; automatic offline queues come later. Sources: [Stripe idempotency design](https://stripe.com/blog/idempotency), [Airbnb double-payment avoidance](https://medium.com/airbnb-engineering/avoiding-double-payments-in-a-distributed-payments-system-2981f6b070bb).

docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:9:Replace the current binary subscription gate (active subscription → full access, no subscription → 402) with a tiered access system that distinguishes four user states: **Paid**, **Trial**, **Expired**, and **Renewal Failed**. Expired and Renewal Failed users retain limited access (10 swap requests/month, restricted chat) rather than being fully blocked. Introduce a hybrid billing model: **monthly recurring** via Telegram's native Star subscriptions (`sendInvoice` with `subscription_period`) and **one-time payment** for multi-month plans (3mo, 6mo) — replacing the current manual three-step purchase flow in both cases. Plan configuration (pricing, duration, billing type, active status) is fully admin-managed. Fill the current subscription analytics blind spot with comprehensive product metrics.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:17:| **Access model** | Binary: has active subscription → full access, else → 402 on swap | 4 tiers: Paid/Trial (full), Expired (limited), Renewal Failed (limited + payment CTA) |
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:18:| **Subscription purchase** | Manual 3-step: `createInvoiceLink` → bot webhook credits `user.balance` → `/api/subscriptions/purchase` debits balance and creates subscription | Hybrid: monthly recurring via `sendInvoice` + `subscription_period`, multi-month (3mo/6mo) via one-time `createInvoiceLink`. Both flows create subscription directly from webhook — no intermediate balance. Plans admin-managed. |
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:19:| **Renewal tracking** | No renewal concept — subscriptions expire silently via cron | `RENEWAL_FAILED` state with `missing_balance` detection, "Update Payment" CTAs |
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:21:| **Chat restrictions** | None — no subscription check on chat creation or messages | Expired users can't initiate chats or send in threads they initiated; can reply in partner threads |

docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:106:**Key:** `billingType` determines the purchase flow. `RECURRING` plans use `sendInvoice` with `subscription_period=2592000` (Telegram only supports 30-day cycles). `ONE_TIME` plans use `createInvoiceLink` for a single payment covering the full `durationMonths`. Pricing and plan configuration are managed entirely from the admin panel — no code changes needed to adjust prices, add/remove plans, or toggle `isActive`.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:108:**Per-bot scoping:** `botId` ties each plan to a specific bot. Users only see plans matching their `registeredBot`. The `@@unique([name, botId])` constraint allows the same plan name (e.g., "Monthly") across different bots with independent pricing. All plan queries must filter by `botId` derived from the authenticated user's `registeredBot`. This includes the trial plan lookup during user registration (`createTrialSubscription`) — the registration flow must select the trial plan matching the user's `registeredBot`, not a global trial plan. Payment handlers must validate `plan.botId === user.registeredBot` before creating invoices or processing payments — this prevents cross-bot plan purchases.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:110:**Admin panel:** Add a `SubscriptionPlan` model config in `apps/admin/src/features/admin/config/models/` with `botId` as a visible, required field (non-editable after creation). Plan list views must be bot-filtered by default. Plan creation requires selecting a bot. Cache invalidation on plan mutation must target the specific bot's cache key (`subscription:plans:{botId}`).
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:123:**Migration:** Add `billingType` column (default `ONE_TIME` for existing plans), `sortOrder` column, `botId` column (nullable initially). Backfill existing plans as `ONE_TIME`. Backfill `botId`: assign all existing plans to bot `am` (default bot). Then duplicate plan rows for additional bots (e.g., `ka`) with the same structure — admin adjusts pricing later. **Existing subscription reassignment:** After duplicating plans for each bot, reassign existing subscriptions from non-`am` users to their bot's corresponding plan: `UPDATE subscription SET "planId" = (SELECT id FROM subscription_plan WHERE name = old_plan.name AND "botId" = user."registeredBot") FROM ... WHERE user."registeredBot" != 'am'`. This ensures each subscription references its bot's plan row. Run a verification query after backfill: `SELECT s.id FROM subscription s JOIN subscription_plan p ON s."planId" = p.id JOIN "user" u ON s."userId" = u.id WHERE p."botId" != u."registeredBot"` — must return zero rows. Make `botId` non-nullable. Add unique constraint `(name, botId)` and index on `botId`. Also add migration note: change `price` column type from `Float` to `Int` — all Stars amounts are whole numbers. Migration: first run verification query `SELECT id, price FROM subscription_plan WHERE price != FLOOR(price);` — if any rows returned, **abort the migration** (do not proceed with truncating fractional prices — fix the data manually first). Then: `ALTER TABLE subscription_plan ALTER COLUMN price TYPE integer USING price::integer;`.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:127:Plans with active subscribers have **immutable billing-critical fields** to prevent mismatches between our DB and Telegram's payment state.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:136:| `price` | Editable | **Locked** — Telegram-side recurring subscription keeps charging the original price; changing DB price creates a mismatch |
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:137:| `billingType` | Editable | **Locked** — existing subscribers have a specific Telegram payment flow; switching is undefined behavior |
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:138:| `durationMonths` | Editable | **Locked** — active OTP subscriptions already have `endDate` set; recurring plans are always 1 month |
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:156:Users with an active subscription are **blocked from purchasing a plan with a different `billingType`**. This prevents billing conflicts between Telegram-side recurring subscriptions and our server-side state.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:162:| No subscription / Expired | Allowed | Allowed |
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:164:| Active `RECURRING` | **Blocked** — Telegram allows one recurring subscription per bot | **Blocked** — "Cancel your monthly subscription first" |
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:167:- **OTP → Recurring:** Telegram starts charging immediately. User has paid OTP time remaining + monthly charges = double-paying for overlap. Telegram has no "start subscription on date X" API.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:168:- **Recurring → OTP:** Old Telegram recurring subscription keeps auto-charging unless explicitly cancelled. Cancellation + new purchase in one flow is error-prone (what if cancellation succeeds but payment fails?).
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:169:- **Recurring → Recurring:** Telegram only allows one active recurring subscription per bot.

docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:399:**Improvement over current 3-step flow:** Both flows eliminate the intermediate `user.balance` wallet. The webhook directly creates/extends the Subscription record — no client-side balance polling or separate purchase API call.

docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:408:- **No pending intent table needed.** The invoice creation itself serves as the pending subscription intent — the `invoice_payload` JSON (`{"userId","planId"}`) is the durable record. Telegram returns this payload verbatim in `successful_payment.invoice_payload`, enabling stateless correlation. No separate "pending intent" table, Redis key, or cleanup job is needed; if the user abandons payment, the invoice simply expires unused.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:409:- **Webhook-side plan drift validation:** On `successful_payment`, the handler must re-read the plan by `planId` from the payload and verify: (1) `successful_payment.total_amount` matches the plan's current `price` — Telegram returns the actual charged amount, so any price change is detectable without payload snapshotting; (2) the plan's `billingType` matches the payment type (inferred from `is_first_recurring`/`is_recurring` flags — if present, the invoice was created for a RECURRING plan). If either check fails (plan edited between invoice creation and payment, possible when `activeSubscriptionCount = 0`), reject the payment with a refund via `refundStarPayment` and prompt the user to retry. This closes the plan term drift window without exceeding the 128-byte payload limit.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:412:**Renewal handling (no separate endpoint):** Renewals arrive via the existing bot webhook (`POST /api/telegram/webhook` → grammY → `bot.on('message:successful_payment')` → `handleSuccessfulPayment` in `payment-event-handler.ts`). The handler dispatches on `is_recurring`/`is_first_recurring` flags per Section 2.4. This avoids creating a separate webhook endpoint — all Telegram payment events are already routed through the bot webhook infrastructure.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:414:**Route wrapper:** `create-star-subscription` uses `createRouteHandler` matching existing subscription routes (e.g., `subscriptions/cancel`, `subscriptions/purchase`).
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:418:Extend `payment-event-handler.ts`.

docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:438:**Idempotency requirement:** The existing `postPurchaseHandler` uses `processWithIdempotency` with `SubscriptionPurchaseIdempotency`. The new recurring payment webhook must have equivalent protection — Telegram may send duplicate `successful_payment` webhooks. Use `telegramChargeId` (unique constraint on `SubscriptionPayment`) as the idempotency key. If a charge ID already exists in `SubscriptionPayment`, return success without side effects.

docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:558:The existing expiration cron (`/api/cron/expire-subscriptions`) has two behaviors that interact with new states:
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:560:1. **Trial mode extension** (`SUBSCRIPTION_TRIAL_MODE_EXTENSION_DAYS`): **Restored** — when enabled (value > 0), extends expiring **trial** subscriptions (only `plan.isTrial = true`) by N days instead of expiring them. Unlike the original implementation which extended ALL subscriptions indiscriminately, this now only targets trial plans. Retained during rollout phase for testing; Phase 6 removal deferred.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:564:3. **New behavior:** The cron must now distinguish trial vs paid subscriptions when expiring:
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:569:4. **`RENEWAL_FAILED` expiry:** The cron should also transition `RENEWAL_FAILED` subscriptions to `EXPIRED` after a configurable grace period (e.g., 7 days past `renewalFailedAt`). This prevents subscriptions from staying in `RENEWAL_FAILED` indefinitely if the user never resolves the payment. The exact grace period is an admin-configurable system setting (`RENEWAL_FAILED_EXPIRY_DAYS`, default: 7). **No additional notification** on `RENEWAL_FAILED → EXPIRED` transition — the user already received the `RENEWAL_FAILED` push notification; sending `SUBSCRIPTION_ENDED` too would be confusing double-notification.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:611:  1. Find latest Subscription for userId via `prisma.subscription.findFirst({ where: { userId }, orderBy: [{ endDate: 'desc' }, { status: 'asc' }] })` — query must include ALL statuses (ACTIVE, CANCELED, RENEWAL_FAILED, EXPIRED), not just ACTIVE. The `findFirst` + `orderBy` implicitly applies `LIMIT 1`.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:612:     — secondary sort `{ status: 'asc' }` is alphabetical: ACTIVE < CANCELED < EXPIRED < RENEWAL_FAILED. This ensures ACTIVE wins over all others for same `endDate`. For the EXPIRED vs RENEWAL_FAILED edge case (same `endDate`, different subscriptions), EXPIRED sorts first — acceptable because same-endDate across distinct subscriptions is extremely unlikely (would require two subscriptions created with identical durations at the same instant). If this proves insufficient, replace with raw SQL `ORDER BY ... CASE status WHEN 'ACTIVE' THEN 1 WHEN 'RENEWAL_FAILED' THEN 2 WHEN 'CANCELED' THEN 3 WHEN 'EXPIRED' THEN 4 END`.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:615:  2. If no subscription → 'LIMITED' (never subscribed = expired trial)
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:625:  6. Check plan.isTrial for trial vs subscription distinction (used by getPendingPaywall for variant)
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:628:**Note on "never subscribed" users:** Step 2 returns `'LIMITED'` for users with no subscription record. This is intentionally equivalent to an expired trial — they receive the same restricted access (swap quota, chat restrictions) as explicitly expired users. The product spec treats "never subscribed" and "expired trial" identically.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:630:**Grace window TTL coherence:** The 24h grace window in step 3 and the cron's 24h window in Section 2.5 must stay in sync. Both use the same 24h value so there is no gap where `getUserAccessLevel` returns `LIMITED` before the cron marks `RENEWAL_FAILED`. The cron invalidates the Redis cache when transitioning a subscription, so cached `FULL` results from the grace window are replaced by the correct `RENEWAL_FAILED` level.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:632:Cache result in Redis with 5-minute TTL, invalidate on subscription change.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:634:**Cache data requirement:** `getUserAccessLevel` needs `billingType` to apply the 24h grace window (step 3). Add `billingType` to `SubscriptionCacheData` in `packages/shared/src/types/subscription.ts` and update `packages/core/src/lib/backend/redis/domains/subscription/` cache population to include it. **Audit all manual `SubscriptionCacheData` construction sites** — any place that builds the cache object literal (e.g., in `set()` callers, webhook handlers, purchase handlers) must include `billingType` to avoid `undefined` in the grace window check. **Also add `billingType` to `SubscriptionStatusResponse`** (CONVENTION:#33) — the client needs it to distinguish recurring vs one-time subscriptions for UI copy and payment recovery flows.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:636:**Cache key version bump (CONVENTION:#48):** Adding `billingType` changes the shape of `SubscriptionCacheData`. Existing cached entries (without `billingType`) will return `undefined` for the field, causing the grace window logic to silently skip. Bump the cache key version in `packages/core/src/lib/backend/redis/constants.ts` where `CACHE_KEYS.SUBSCRIPTION` is defined (e.g., from `subscription:` to `subscription:v2:`) — note: `domains/subscription/constants.ts` re-exports this value, so bumping at the source propagates automatically so stale entries are ignored. Alternatively, flush all subscription cache keys during deployment migration.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:638:**Per-bot plan cache keys:** The subscription plans cache must be bot-scoped. Change the global `SUBSCRIPTION_PLANS_KEY` to a parameterized key: `subscription:plans:{botId}` (e.g., `subscription:plans:am`, `subscription:plans:ka`). Update `getPlans(botId)`, `setPlans(botId, plans)`, and `invalidatePlans(botId)` to accept `botId` as a parameter. The `getBatched()` pipeline optimization that retrieves plans and user data in one call must also pass `botId` for the plans leg.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:640:**Client-side state invalidation (CONVENTION:#31):** When subscription status transitions occur server-side (webhook renewal, cron expiry, RENEWAL_FAILED detection), the client Zustand store holds stale access level data until the next API call. Options: (a) add a Pusher event (`subscription:status_changed`) fired on every server-side status transition, with the client store subscribing and refetching; (b) poll `/api/subscriptions/status` on a short interval when the subscription is near expiry. Option (a) is preferred for real-time accuracy. Without this, users with the app open during a RENEWAL_FAILED transition continue seeing full-access UI until they navigate or refresh.

docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:922:Client calls `getPendingPaywall` on app open. The server records the `MonetizationUiEvent` with `relatedAt` set to the lapse boundary (e.g., `subscription.endDate` or `subscription.renewalFailedAt`) **before** returning the variant to the client — this ensures the event is persisted even if the client crashes before rendering the modal.
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:924:**Re-expiry cycle handling:** When a user re-subscribes and their new subscription expires again, the new `endDate` is later than the old event's `relatedAt`. The comparison `lastEvent.relatedAt < subscription.endDate` evaluates to `true`, re-triggering the paywall for the new lapse. No event cleanup or deletion is needed — the `relatedAt` timestamp naturally scopes each event to its originating lapse.

docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:986:**Important:** The current `NotificationLog` has `@@unique([userId, notificationType])`, allowing only one row per user per type. This prevents re-sending notifications on re-expiry cycles (user subscribes again, expires again). Migration must either: (a) drop the unique constraint and add a `subscriptionId` column for per-lapse deduplication (matching the `MonetizationUiEvent` pattern), or (b) use `upsert` to update the existing row's `sentAt` when re-triggering. Option (a) is preferred for audit trail. Add migration: drop unique index, add `subscriptionId` column (nullable — some notifications like `TRIAL_ENDED` for never-subscribed users may not have a subscriptionId), create new composite unique index. **PostgreSQL NULL semantics:** `@@unique([userId, notificationType, subscriptionId])` allows multiple rows with the same `(userId, notificationType)` when `subscriptionId IS NULL` because PostgreSQL treats each NULL as distinct in unique constraints. If per-lapse deduplication is needed for NULL-subscriptionId cases, use a partial unique index: `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ... ON notification_log(userId, notificationType) WHERE subscriptionId IS NULL` in addition to the composite unique for non-NULL cases.

docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:1077:**Undo/reset:** All simulation actions are reversible. "Simulate Expiry" and "Simulate Renewal Failure" can be undone with "Restore Subscription" + "Clear Paywall Events". The admin panel shows a warning before simulation: "This will change the user's subscription state. Use 'Restore Subscription' to undo."
docs/rfcs/RFC-046-new-monetization-logic.md:1082:- Server-side `endDate` calculation uses 1-minute duration instead of 30 days (for rapid iteration). Note: `subscription_period` sent to Telegram API remains 2592000 (Telegram's only accepted value). The shortened duration only affects our DB `endDate`, causing faster expiration/renewal-failure detection locally.

docs/rfcs/RFC-038-idempotency-cleanup-optimization.md:9:The `cleanup-idempotency` cron job runs daily at 2 AM UTC on Vercel (`vercel.json`: `"0 2 * * *"`) to delete expired and stalled rows from the `subscription_purchase_idempotency` Postgres table. Traces show it takes **4.6 seconds** per invocation, almost entirely due to serverless cold start overhead (loading Next.js + Prisma) for what amounts to two simple `DELETE` queries.
docs/rfcs/RFC-038-idempotency-cleanup-optimization.md:11:The table exists to prevent duplicate subscription purchases using a claim-and-poll idempotency pattern. Records have a 24-hour TTL (`expiresAt` column) and can get stuck in a `202` processing state if the claiming process crashes. The cron cleans up both cases.

docs/product-reqs/Monetization Model and Flow.md:3:Every new user **receives Free Tomes**(Chapta’s in-app currency), allowing them to unlock a **free trial subscription** and experience **unlimited matches & chat** for a limited time. After the subscription ends, they must either **subscribe with Tomes or invite friends** to continue swapping.

docs/product-reqs/Monetization Model and Flow.md:16:Tapping “Get X Tomes” opens a Telegram payment screen, allowing the user to purchase the exact number of **Telegram Stars** needed.
docs/product-reqs/Monetization Model and Flow.md:24:* When a user tries to send a swap request, they see a **subscription page, but a bit different than the usual subscription page.**  
docs/product-reqs/Monetization Model and Flow.md:27:* Once subscribed, they get **unlimited chat & matches** for the duration of their subscription.
docs/product-reqs/Monetization Model and Flow.md:33:* When their subscription expires, they **lose access to new matches** and **can’t send swap requests**.

docs/solutions/test-failures/non-idempotent-seeder-blocks-e2e-suite-20260313.md:8:  - "Prisma unique constraint violation on subscription.create() during global setup"
docs/solutions/test-failures/non-idempotent-seeder-blocks-e2e-suite-20260313.md:21:The entire E2E test suite (168 tests) failed with 0 passing. The global setup seeder crashed on `prisma.subscription.create()` with a unique constraint violation, preventing any tests from running. The constraint was a **partial unique index** defined in a raw SQL migration — invisible in the Prisma schema.

docs/solutions/test-failures/non-idempotent-seeder-blocks-e2e-suite-20260313.md:75:1. **Root cause:** The `seed-subscriptions.ts` seeder used raw `create()` without checking for existing data. A partial unique index (`unique_active_subscription_per_user`) enforced one active subscription per user at the database level, but this constraint was only in a raw SQL migration — not in the Prisma schema, so no type-level warning.
docs/solutions/test-failures/non-idempotent-seeder-blocks-e2e-suite-20260313.md:77:2. **Why delete-before-create works:** It ensures idempotency. Whether the seeder runs once or many times, the result is the same: exactly one active subscription for the test user.

import { NextRequest } from 'next/server';
import { z } from 'zod';
import { prisma } from '@backend/prisma';
import { BillingType, SubscriptionStatus } from '@generated/prisma/client';
import { jsonResponse, errorResponse, handleAuthError } from '@root/lib/api/responseHelpers';
import { getLoggerForRequest } from '@root/lib/logger/logger.server';
import { authenticateRequest } from '@backend/auth';
import { checkSubscriptionRateLimit } from '@/features/subscription/api/subscriptionRateLimit';
import { bot } from '@/lib/telegram/bot';
import { getMessage, getValidLocale } from '@backend/i18n';
import { StarSubscriptionErrorCode } from './types';

const TELEGRAM_PAYLOAD_MAX_BYTES = 128;
const RECURRING_SUBSCRIPTION_PERIOD_SECONDS = 2592000; // 30 days

const createStarSubscriptionSchema = z.object({
  planId: z.string().min(1),
});

export async function postCreateStarSubscription(request: NextRequest) {
  const log = getLoggerForRequest(request);

  try {
    const user = await authenticateRequest(request);
    const userId = user.id;

    const isRateLimited = await checkSubscriptionRateLimit(userId);
    if (isRateLimited) {
      return errorResponse(
        request,
        { errorCode: StarSubscriptionErrorCode.RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED, message: 'Rate limit exceeded' },
        429,
      );
    }

    let body;
    try {
      body = await request.json();
    } catch {
      return errorResponse(
        request,
        { errorCode: StarSubscriptionErrorCode.PROCESSING_ERROR, message: 'Invalid JSON' },
        400,
      );
    }

    const validation = createStarSubscriptionSchema.safeParse(body);
    if (!validation.success) {
      return errorResponse(
        request,
        { errorCode: StarSubscriptionErrorCode.PROCESSING_ERROR, message: 'Validation failed' },
        400,
      );
    }

    const { planId } = validation.data;

    const plan = await prisma.subscriptionPlan.findUnique({
      where: { id: planId, isActive: true },
    });

    if (!plan) {
      return errorResponse(
        request,
        { errorCode: StarSubscriptionErrorCode.PLAN_NOT_FOUND, message: 'Plan not found or inactive' },
        404,
      );
    }

    if (plan.botId !== user.registeredBot) {
      return errorResponse(
        request,
        { errorCode: StarSubscriptionErrorCode.PLAN_NOT_FOUND, message: 'Plan not found or inactive' },
        404,
      );
    }

    if (plan.planType !== 'STANDARD') {
      return errorResponse(
        request,
        { errorCode: StarSubscriptionErrorCode.NON_STANDARD_PLAN, message: 'Only standard plans can be purchased' },
        400,
      );
    }

    // Plan transition validation (RFC-046 Section 1.2.2)
    // Check both ACTIVE and CANCELED-with-remaining-access subscriptions
    const now = new Date();
    const activeSubscription = await prisma.subscription.findFirst({
      where: {
        userId,
        status: { in: [SubscriptionStatus.ACTIVE, SubscriptionStatus.CANCELED] },
        endDate: { gt: now },
      },
      orderBy: { endDate: 'desc' },
    });

    if (activeSubscription) {
      if (activeSubscription.billingType === BillingType.RECURRING) {
        return errorResponse(
          request,
          {
            errorCode: StarSubscriptionErrorCode.ACTIVE_RECURRING_EXISTS,
            message: 'Cannot purchase while a recurring subscription is active',
            currentBillingType: activeSubscription.billingType,
            currentPlanEndDate: activeSubscription.endDate.toISOString(),
          },
          409,
        );
      }

      if (plan.billingType === BillingType.RECURRING) {
        return errorResponse(
          request,
          {
            errorCode: StarSubscriptionErrorCode.BILLING_TYPE_MISMATCH,
            message: 'Cannot upgrade from one-time to recurring while subscription is active',
            currentBillingType: activeSubscription.billingType,
            currentPlanEndDate: activeSubscription.endDate.toISOString(),
          },
          409,
        );
      }
    }

    // Build invoice payload
    const invoicePayload = JSON.stringify({ userId, planId, ts: Date.now() });
    if (Buffer.byteLength(invoicePayload, 'utf8') > TELEGRAM_PAYLOAD_MAX_BYTES) {
      log.error('Invoice payload exceeds Telegram limit', { payloadLength: Buffer.byteLength(invoicePayload, 'utf8') });
      return errorResponse(
        request,
        { errorCode: StarSubscriptionErrorCode.PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE, message: 'Invoice payload too large' },
        500,
      );
    }

    // Translate plan name for Telegram invoice display
    const userLocale = getValidLocale(user.languageCode);
    const planTitle = getMessage({ locale: userLocale, path: plan.name });

    // Create Telegram Stars invoice link
    try {
      const invoiceUrl = await bot.api.createInvoiceLink(
        planTitle,
        planTitle,
        invoicePayload,
        '',
        'XTR',
        [{ label: planTitle, amount: plan.price }],
        plan.billingType === BillingType.RECURRING
          ? { subscription_period: RECURRING_SUBSCRIPTION_PERIOD_SECONDS }
          : {},
      );

      log.info('Star subscription invoice created', { userId, planId, billingType: plan.billingType });

      return jsonResponse(request, { success: true, invoiceUrl }, 200);
    } catch (invoiceError) {
      log.error('Failed to create Telegram invoice link', { error: invoiceError, userId, planId });
      return errorResponse(
        request,
        { errorCode: StarSubscriptionErrorCode.INVOICE_CREATION_FAILED, message: 'Failed to create invoice' },
        500,
      );
    }
  } catch (error) {
    const authResponse = handleAuthError(request, error);
    if (authResponse) return authResponse;

    log.error('Star subscription creation error', { error });
    return errorResponse(
      request,
      { errorCode: StarSubscriptionErrorCode.PROCESSING_ERROR, message: 'Processing error' },
      500,
    );
  }
}

model feedback {
  id               String   @id @default(uuid())
  rating           Int      @db.SmallInt // 1-5 star rating
  text             String? // Optional text feedback
  isPublic         Boolean  @default(false) // Whether to show in public profile
  notificationSent Boolean  @default(false) // Whether notification was sent
  createdAt        DateTime @default(now())
  swapId           String // Link feedback to a specific swap
  swap             swap     @relation(fields: [swapId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  user             user?    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
  userId           String?

  @@index([swapId])
  @@index([isPublic])
  @@map("feedback")
}

model SubscriptionPlan {
  id             String         @id @default(cuid())
  name           String
  botId          String?        // Nullable for generic mobile plans (botId: null)
  durationDays   Int
  price          Int
  billingType    BillingType    @default(ONE_TIME)
  isActive       Boolean        @default(true)
  planType       PlanType       @default(STANDARD)
  sortOrder      Int            @default(0)
  createdAt      DateTime       @default(now())
  updatedAt      DateTime       @updatedAt
  bot                  Bot?                   @relation(fields: [botId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
  subscriptions        Subscription[]
  referralRewardGrants referral_reward_grant[]

  // Uniqueness enforced via partial unique indexes in raw SQL (Prisma can't express partial indexes)
  // subscription_plan_name_botId_key: UNIQUE(name, botId) WHERE botId IS NOT NULL
  // subscription_plan_name_generic_key: UNIQUE(name) WHERE botId IS NULL
  @@index([name, botId])
  @@index([botId])
  @@map("subscription_plan")
}

model Subscription {
  id                     String             @id @default(cuid())
  userId                 String
  planId                 String
  startDate              DateTime           @default(now())
  endDate                DateTime
  status                 SubscriptionStatus
  billingType            BillingType        @default(ONE_TIME)
  telegramSubscriptionId String?
  telegramChargeId       String?
  providerChargeId       String?
  lastRenewalAt          DateTime?
  renewalFailedAt        DateTime?
  createdAt              DateTime           @default(now())
  updatedAt              DateTime           @updatedAt

  user                 user                   @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  plan                 SubscriptionPlan       @relation(fields: [planId], references: [id])
  payments             SubscriptionPayment[]
  referralRewardGrants referral_reward_grant[]

  @@index([userId])
  @@index([planId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([endDate])
  @@index([telegramSubscriptionId])
  @@index([billingType, status, endDate])
  @@index([userId, status])
  @@index([userId, endDate])
  @@map("subscription")
}

model SubscriptionPayment {
  id               String   @id @default(cuid())
  subscriptionId   String
  telegramChargeId String   @unique
  providerChargeId String?
  amount           Int
  currency         String   @default("XTR")
  paidAt           DateTime @default(now())
  createdAt        DateTime @default(now())

  subscription Subscription @relation(fields: [subscriptionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([subscriptionId])
  @@map("subscription_payment")
}

model userFavoriteBook {
  id         String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId     String
  userbookId String
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
  user       user     @relation("userFavorites", fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  userbook   userbook @relation(fields: [userbookId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([userId, userbookId])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([userbookId])
  @@map("user_favorite_book")
}

model SubscriptionPurchaseIdempotency {
  id             String   @id @default(uuid())
  idempotencyKey String   @unique
  userId         String
  // NOTE: planId is intentionally NOT a foreign key relation to SubscriptionPlan.
  // This temporary table (24hr expiry) should be decoupled from plan lifecycle.
  // Idempotency records must outlive deleted plans to handle retries correctly.
  planId         String
  responseStatus Int
  responseBody   Json
  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())
  expiresAt      DateTime

  user user @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([userId])
  @@index([idempotencyKey])
  @@index([expiresAt])
  @@map("subscription_purchase_idempotency")
}

import logger from '@root/lib/logger/logger.server';
import { prisma } from '@root/lib/backend/prisma';
import redis from '@backend/redis/index';
import { monetizationPolicyService } from '@backend/monetization/MonetizationPolicyService';
import { SubscriptionStatus, BillingType, Prisma } from '@generated/prisma/client';
import { isSubscriptionTestMode } from '@backend/settings';
import { createOrExtendSubscription } from '@backend/subscriptions';
import { sendNotificationToUser } from '@root/lib/backend/notifications/sender';
import { getNotificationConfig } from '@root/lib/backend/notifications/config';
import { getMessage, getValidLocale } from '@root/lib/backend/i18n';
import { emitSubscriptionStatusChanged } from '@/lib/pusher-server';
import { bot } from '@/lib/telegram/bot';
import { AnalyticsService } from '@/lib/analytics/service';
import { AnalyticsEvent } from '@/lib/analytics/types';
import { fireAttributedCAPIEvent } from '@backend/meta/capi';

const analyticsService = new AnalyticsService();

interface ExpiredRenewalFailedSub {
  id: string;
  billingType: BillingType;
}

export interface SubscriptionPaymentData {
  userId: string;
  planId: string;
  telegramChargeId: string;
  providerChargeId?: string;
  totalAmount: number;
  isFirstRecurring: boolean;
  isRecurring: boolean;
  subscriptionExpirationDate?: number;
}

export interface SubscriptionPaymentResult {
  status: 'created' | 'renewed' | 'extended' | 'already_processed';
  subscriptionId?: string;
}

export async function processSubscriptionPayment(
  data: SubscriptionPaymentData,
): Promise<SubscriptionPaymentResult> {
  const { planId, telegramChargeId, totalAmount, isFirstRecurring, isRecurring } = data;

  // Idempotency: check if this charge was already processed
  const existingPayment = await prisma.subscriptionPayment.findUnique({
    where: { telegramChargeId },
  });

  if (existingPayment) {
    logger.info('Subscription payment already processed', { telegramChargeId, subscriptionId: existingPayment.subscriptionId });
    return { status: 'already_processed', subscriptionId: existingPayment.subscriptionId };
  }

  const plan = await prisma.subscriptionPlan.findUnique({
    where: { id: planId },
  });

  if (!plan) {
    analyticsService.track(AnalyticsEvent.PURCHASE_FAILED, {
      plan_id: planId,
      billing_type: isRecurring ? 'RECURRING' : 'ONE_TIME',
      error_type: 'plan_not_found',
    }, data.userId).catch(() => {});
    throw new Error(`Subscription plan not found: ${planId}`);
  }

  const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
    where: { id: data.userId },
    select: { registeredBot: true },
  });
  if (!user || plan.botId !== user.registeredBot) {
    throw new Error(`Cross-bot plan purchase rejected: plan ${planId} belongs to bot ${plan.botId}, user registered with ${user?.registeredBot}`);
  }

  if (plan.price !== totalAmount) {
    logger.warn('Plan drift: price mismatch', { planId, expected: plan.price, received: totalAmount, telegramChargeId });
    analyticsService.track(AnalyticsEvent.PURCHASE_FAILED, {
      plan_id: planId,
      billing_type: isRecurring ? 'RECURRING' : 'ONE_TIME',
      error_type: 'price_mismatch',
    }, data.userId).catch(() => {});
    throw new Error(`Plan drift: expected ${plan.price}, received ${totalAmount}`);
  }

  if (isRecurring && plan.billingType !== BillingType.RECURRING) {
    logger.warn('Plan drift: billingType mismatch', { planId, planBillingType: plan.billingType, telegramChargeId });
    analyticsService.track(AnalyticsEvent.PURCHASE_FAILED, {
      plan_id: planId,
      billing_type: 'RECURRING',
      error_type: 'billing_type_mismatch',
    }, data.userId).catch(() => {});
    throw new Error(`Plan drift: recurring payment for non-recurring plan ${planId}`);
  }

  // M9: Reverse drift — one-time payment but plan is now recurring
  if (!isRecurring && !isFirstRecurring && plan.billingType === BillingType.RECURRING) {
    logger.warn('Plan drift: one-time payment for recurring plan', { planId, planBillingType: plan.billingType, telegramChargeId });
    analyticsService.track(AnalyticsEvent.PURCHASE_FAILED, {
      plan_id: planId,
      billing_type: 'ONE_TIME',
      error_type: 'billing_type_mismatch',
    }, data.userId).catch(() => {});
    throw new Error(`Plan drift: one-time payment for recurring plan ${planId}`);
  }

  if (isFirstRecurring) {
    return handleFirstRecurringPayment(data, plan);
  }

  if (isRecurring) {
    return handleRenewalPayment(data);
  }

  return handleOneTimePayment(data, plan);
}

async function handleFirstRecurringPayment(
  data: SubscriptionPaymentData,
  plan: { id: string; name: string; durationDays: number; planType: string; price: number },
): Promise<SubscriptionPaymentResult> {
  const { userId, telegramChargeId, providerChargeId, totalAmount, subscriptionExpirationDate } = data;
  const now = new Date();

  const testMode = await isSubscriptionTestMode();
  const endDate = testMode
    ? new Date(now.getTime() + 60 * 1000) // 1 minute in test mode
    : subscriptionExpirationDate
      ? new Date(subscriptionExpirationDate * 1000)
      : new Date(now.getTime() + plan.durationDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);

  let expiredRenewalFailedSub: ExpiredRenewalFailedSub | null = null;

  try {
    const result = await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {
      // Expire any RENEWAL_FAILED subscriptions to avoid ambiguity
      const renewalFailedSubs = await tx.subscription.findMany({
        where: { userId, status: SubscriptionStatus.RENEWAL_FAILED },
        select: { id: true, billingType: true },
      });

      if (renewalFailedSubs.length > 0) {
        await tx.subscription.updateMany({
          where: { userId, status: SubscriptionStatus.RENEWAL_FAILED },
          data: { status: SubscriptionStatus.EXPIRED },
        });
        expiredRenewalFailedSub = renewalFailedSubs[0];
        logger.info('Expired RENEWAL_FAILED subscriptions before new purchase', {
          userId,
          expiredIds: renewalFailedSubs.map((s) => s.id),
        });
      }

      const subscription = await tx.subscription.create({
        data: {
          userId,
          planId: plan.id,
          status: SubscriptionStatus.ACTIVE,
          billingType: BillingType.RECURRING,
          startDate: now,
          endDate,
          telegramChargeId,
        },
      });

      await tx.subscriptionPayment.create({
        data: {
          subscriptionId: subscription.id,
          telegramChargeId,
          providerChargeId: providerChargeId ?? null,
          amount: totalAmount,
          paidAt: now,
        },
      });

      return subscription;
    });

    // Post-commit: cancel old recurring subscription on Telegram side
    await cancelExpiredRenewalFailedOnTelegram(userId, expiredRenewalFailedSub);

    // Post-commit side effects — must not throw to avoid triggering refund after successful DB commit
    try {
      await invalidateUserCaches(userId);
      emitSubscriptionStatusChanged(userId, { status: 'ACTIVE', accessLevel: 'FULL' });

      const isFirstPurchase = (await prisma.subscription.count({ where: { userId } })) === 1;

      analyticsService.track(AnalyticsEvent.PURCHASE_SUCCEEDED, {
        plan_id: plan.id,
        subscription_id: result.id,
        billing_type: 'RECURRING',
        price: totalAmount,
        is_first_purchase: isFirstPurchase,
      }, userId).catch((e) => logger.error('Analytics track error', { error: e }));

      // Fire Meta CAPI Subscribe event for ad-attributed users
      void fireMetaCAPISubscribe(userId, totalAmount, telegramChargeId);

      // Send SUBSCRIPTION_ACTIVATED notification
      await sendSubscriptionActivatedNotification(userId, plan.name, endDate).catch((e) =>
        logger.error('Failed to send subscription activated notification', { error: e, userId }),
      );
    } catch (sideEffectError) {
      logger.error('Post-commit side effect failed (subscription committed successfully)', {
        error: sideEffectError instanceof Error ? sideEffectError.message : String(sideEffectError),
        subscriptionId: result.id, userId,
      });
    }

    logger.info('Created recurring subscription', {
      subscriptionId: result.id,
      userId,
      planId: plan.id,
      endDate,
      testMode,
    });

    return { status: 'created', subscriptionId: result.id };
  } catch (error: unknown) {
    // P2002 = unique constraint violation → idempotent success
    const isPrismaError = error instanceof Prisma.PrismaClientKnownRequestError;
    const prismaError = isPrismaError ? (error as Prisma.PrismaClientKnownRequestError) : null;
    if (prismaError?.code === 'P2002') {
      logger.info('Duplicate recurring subscription creation caught by unique index', { userId, telegramChargeId, target: prismaError.meta?.target });
      const existing = await prisma.subscription.findFirst({
        where: { userId, status: SubscriptionStatus.ACTIVE, billingType: BillingType.RECURRING },
      });
      return { status: 'already_processed', subscriptionId: existing?.id };
    }
    throw error;
  }
}

import { NextRequest } from 'next/server';
import { z } from 'zod';
import { prisma } from '@backend/prisma';
import { BillingType, SubscriptionStatus } from '@generated/prisma/client';
import { bot } from '@/lib/telegram/bot';
import { getMessage } from '@backend/i18n';
import { jsonResponse, handleAuthError } from '@root/lib/api/responseHelpers';
import { getLoggerForRequest } from '@root/lib/logger/logger.server';
import { authenticateRequest } from '@backend/auth';
import { SubscriptionCancellationErrorCode } from './types';
import { monetizationPolicyService } from '@backend/monetization/MonetizationPolicyService';
import { emitSubscriptionStatusChanged } from '@/lib/pusher-server';
// Schema for subscription cancellation validation
const cancelSchema = z.object({
  subscriptionId: z.string().min(1, 'Subscription ID is required'),
});

/**
 * Handler for POST /api/subscriptions/cancel
 * Cancels an active subscription for the authenticated user
 */
export async function cancelSubscriptionHandler(request: NextRequest) {
  // Create a request-scoped logger
  const log = getLoggerForRequest(request);
  try {
    // Validate the user first
    const user = await authenticateRequest(request);
    const authenticatedUserId = user.id; // Get ID from the authenticated user

    // Parse and validate the request data
    let body;
    try {
      body = await request.json();
    } catch (jsonError) {
      log.error('Invalid JSON in cancel subscription request:', { error: jsonError });
      return jsonResponse(
        request,
        {
          success: false,
          errorCode: SubscriptionCancellationErrorCode.INVALID_DATA,
          message: getMessage({ locale: 'en', path: 'subscription.errors.invalidData' }),
          details: [{ message: 'Invalid JSON body' }],
        },
        400,
      );
    }

    // Validate using Zod schema (only subscriptionId needed now)
    const validationResult = cancelSchema.safeParse(body);
    if (!validationResult.success) {
      log.error('Subscription cancellation validation error:', { error: validationResult.error });
      return jsonResponse(
        request,
        {
          success: false,
          errorCode: SubscriptionCancellationErrorCode.INVALID_DATA,
          message: getMessage({ locale: 'en', path: 'subscription.errors.invalidData' }),
          details: validationResult.error.errors,
        },
        400,
      );
    }

    const { subscriptionId } = validationResult.data;

    // Verify subscription exists and belongs to the *authenticated* user
    const subscription = await prisma.subscription.findFirst({
      where: {
        id: subscriptionId,
        userId: authenticatedUserId, // Use the ID from authentication
        status: SubscriptionStatus.ACTIVE,
      },
      include: {
        plan: true,
      },
    });

    if (!subscription) {
      return jsonResponse(
        request,
        {
          success: false,
          errorCode: SubscriptionCancellationErrorCode.NOT_FOUND,
          message: getMessage({ locale: 'en', path: 'subscription.errors.notFoundOrCanceled' }),
        },
        404,
      );
    }

    try {
      // Update subscription status
      const updatedSubscription = await prisma.subscription.update({
        where: { id: subscriptionId },
        data: { status: SubscriptionStatus.CANCELED },
      });

      // Cancel Telegram Stars recurring subscription via Bot API
      if (subscription.billingType === BillingType.RECURRING && user.telegramId) {
        try {
          // Get initial charge ID (first payment, not latest)
          const initialPayment = await prisma.subscriptionPayment.findFirst({
            where: { subscriptionId: subscription.id },
            orderBy: { paidAt: 'asc' },
          });

          if (initialPayment) {
            await (bot.api as any).editUserStarSubscription(
              Number(user.telegramId),
              initialPayment.telegramChargeId,
              { is_canceled: true },
            );
            log.info('Telegram Stars subscription canceled via Bot API', {
              subscriptionId,
              telegramChargeId: initialPayment.telegramChargeId,
            });
          }
        } catch (botError) {
          // Log but don't fail — local cancellation already succeeded
          log.error('Failed to cancel Telegram Stars subscription via Bot API', {
            error: botError instanceof Error ? botError.message : String(botError),
            subscriptionId,
          });
        }
      }

      // Invalidate Redis cache for the authenticated user
      await monetizationPolicyService.invalidateAllCaches(authenticatedUserId);
      emitSubscriptionStatusChanged(authenticatedUserId, { status: 'CANCELED', accessLevel: 'FULL' });

      return jsonResponse(
        request,
        {
          success: true,
          message: getMessage({ locale: 'en', path: 'subscription.messages.cancelSuccess' }),
          subscription: {
            id: updatedSubscription.id,
            status: updatedSubscription.status,
            endDate: updatedSubscription.endDate.toISOString(),
          },
        },
        200,
      );
    } catch (updateError) {
      log.error('Subscription update failed during cancellation:', { error: updateError });
      return jsonResponse(
        request,
        {
          success: false,
          errorCode: SubscriptionCancellationErrorCode.CANCEL_FAILED,
          message: getMessage({ locale: 'en', path: 'subscription.errors.cancelFailed' }),
        },
        500,
      );
    }
  } catch (error) {
    const authResponse = handleAuthError(request, error);
    if (authResponse) return authResponse;
    log.error('Subscription cancellation processing error:', { error });
    return jsonResponse(
      request,
      {
        success: false,
        errorCode: SubscriptionCancellationErrorCode.PROCESSING_ERROR,
        message: getMessage({ locale: 'en', path: 'subscription.errors.cancelFailed' }),
      },
      500,
    );
  }
}

async function handleOneTimePayment(
  data: SubscriptionPaymentData,
  plan: { id: string; name: string; durationDays: number; planType: string; price: number },
): Promise<SubscriptionPaymentResult> {
  const { userId, telegramChargeId, providerChargeId, totalAmount } = data;
  const now = new Date();

  let subscriptionResult: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof createOrExtendSubscription>>;

  try {
    subscriptionResult = await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {
      const result = await createOrExtendSubscription(
        tx,
        userId,
        plan.durationDays,
        plan.id,
        BillingType.ONE_TIME,
      );

      // Set telegramChargeId on newly created subscriptions
      if (result.operation === 'created') {
        await tx.subscription.update({
          where: { id: result.subscriptionId },
          data: { telegramChargeId },
        });
      }

      // Record the payment
      await tx.subscriptionPayment.create({
        data: {
          subscriptionId: result.subscriptionId,
          telegramChargeId,
          providerChargeId: providerChargeId ?? null,
          amount: totalAmount,
          paidAt: now,
        },
      });

      return result;
    });
  } catch (error: unknown) {
    const isPrismaError = error instanceof Prisma.PrismaClientKnownRequestError;
    const prismaError = isPrismaError ? (error as Prisma.PrismaClientKnownRequestError) : null;
    if (prismaError?.code === 'P2002') {
      logger.info('Duplicate one-time payment caught by unique index', { userId, telegramChargeId, target: prismaError.meta?.target });
      const existing = await prisma.subscription.findFirst({
        where: { userId, status: SubscriptionStatus.ACTIVE },
      });
      return { status: 'already_processed', subscriptionId: existing?.id };
    }
    throw error;
  }

  // Post-commit: cancel old recurring subscription on Telegram side
  const expiredRenewalFailedSub = subscriptionResult.expiredRenewalFailedSubs[0] ?? null;
  await cancelExpiredRenewalFailedOnTelegram(userId, expiredRenewalFailedSub);

  // Post-commit side effects — must not throw to avoid triggering refund after successful DB commit
  try {
    await invalidateUserCaches(userId);
    emitSubscriptionStatusChanged(userId, { status: 'ACTIVE', accessLevel: 'FULL' });

    const isFirstPurchase = subscriptionResult.operation === 'created'
      && (await prisma.subscription.count({ where: { userId } })) === 1;

    analyticsService.track(AnalyticsEvent.PURCHASE_SUCCEEDED, {
      plan_id: plan.id,
      subscription_id: subscriptionResult.subscriptionId,
      billing_type: 'ONE_TIME',
      price: totalAmount,
      is_first_purchase: isFirstPurchase,
    }, userId).catch((e) => logger.error('Analytics track error', { error: e }));

    // Fire Meta CAPI Subscribe event for ad-attributed users
    void fireMetaCAPISubscribe(userId, totalAmount, telegramChargeId);

    // Send SUBSCRIPTION_ACTIVATED notification
    await sendSubscriptionActivatedNotification(userId, plan.name, subscriptionResult.newEndDate).catch((e) =>
      logger.error('Failed to send subscription activated notification', { error: e, userId }),
    );
  } catch (sideEffectError) {
    logger.error('Post-commit side effect failed (one-time subscription committed successfully)', {
      error: sideEffectError instanceof Error ? sideEffectError.message : String(sideEffectError),
      subscriptionId: subscriptionResult.subscriptionId, userId,
    });
  }

  const paymentStatus: SubscriptionPaymentResult['status'] =
    subscriptionResult.operation === 'created' ? 'created' : 'extended';

  return { status: paymentStatus, subscriptionId: subscriptionResult.subscriptionId };
}

async function cancelExpiredRenewalFailedOnTelegram(
  userId: string,
  expiredSub: ExpiredRenewalFailedSub | null,
): Promise<void> {
  if (!expiredSub || expiredSub.billingType !== BillingType.RECURRING) return;

  try {
    const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
      where: { id: userId },
      select: { telegramId: true },
    });

    if (!user?.telegramId) return;

    const initialPayment = await prisma.subscriptionPayment.findFirst({
      where: { subscriptionId: expiredSub.id },
      orderBy: { paidAt: 'asc' },
    });

    if (!initialPayment) return;

    await (bot.api as any).editUserStarSubscription(
      Number(user.telegramId),
      initialPayment.telegramChargeId,
      { is_canceled: true },
    );

    logger.info('Canceled old RENEWAL_FAILED recurring subscription on Telegram', {
      userId,
      oldSubscriptionId: expiredSub.id,
      telegramChargeId: initialPayment.telegramChargeId,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    // Don't fail the whole flow if Telegram cancel fails
    logger.error('Failed to cancel old RENEWAL_FAILED subscription on Telegram', {
      error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
      userId,
      oldSubscriptionId: expiredSub.id,
    });
  }

  // Invalidate cache for old subscription state
  await invalidateUserCaches(userId).catch((e) =>
    logger.error('Failed to invalidate caches for expired RENEWAL_FAILED sub', { error: e }),
  );
}

async function invalidateUserCaches(userId: string): Promise<void> {
  await Promise.all([
    monetizationPolicyService.invalidateAllCaches(userId),
    redis.operations.invalidateAllUserData(userId),
  ]);
}

async function sendSubscriptionActivatedNotification(
  userId: string,
  planName: string,
  endDate: Date,
): Promise<void> {
  const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
    where: { id: userId },
    select: { telegramId: true, languageCode: true, registeredBot: true },
  });

  if (!user?.telegramId) return;

  const userLocale = getValidLocale(user.languageCode);
  // Resolve plan name from i18n key (e.g. 'subscription.plan.1month' → '1 Month')
  const resolvedPlanName = getMessage({ locale: userLocale, path: planName });
  const expirationDate = endDate.toLocaleDateString(userLocale, {
    year: 'numeric',
    month: 'short',
    day: 'numeric',
  });

  const config = getNotificationConfig('SUBSCRIPTION_ACTIVATED');
  await sendNotificationToUser({
    userId,
    telegramId: user.telegramId,
    languageCode: user.languageCode,
    title: config.titleKey,
    message: config.messageKey,
    replacements: { planName: resolvedPlanName, expirationDate },
    buttons: config.buttons.map((btn) => ({
      text: btn.textKey,
      action: btn.action,
      url: btn.urlPattern,
    })),
    notificationType: 'subscription_activated',
    tier: 'TRANSACTIONAL',
    registeredBot: user.registeredBot ?? undefined,
  });
}

async function fireMetaCAPISubscribe(userId: string, totalAmount: number, telegramChargeId: string): Promise<void> {
  const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
    where: { id: userId },
    select: { telegramId: true },
  });
  if (!user?.telegramId) return;
  await fireAttributedCAPIEvent(
    userId,
    user.telegramId.toString(),
    'Subscribe',
    { customData: { value: totalAmount, currency: 'XTR' }, dedupSuffix: telegramChargeId },
  );
}

import { SubscriptionStatus, BillingType, Prisma } from '@generated/prisma/client';
import { isSubscriptionTestMode } from '@/lib/backend/settings';
import logger from '@/lib/logger/logger.server';

type TransactionClient = Prisma.TransactionClient;

export interface CreateOrExtendSubscriptionResult {
  subscriptionId: string;
  previousEndDate: Date | null;
  newEndDate: Date;
  operation: 'created' | 'extended' | 'reactivated';
  previousStatus: SubscriptionStatus | null;
  newStatus: SubscriptionStatus;
  /** RENEWAL_FAILED subscriptions that were expired as part of entitlement resolution */
  expiredRenewalFailedSubs: Array<{ id: string; billingType: BillingType }>;
}

interface CreateOrExtendSubscriptionOptions {
  /** When true, CANCELED subscriptions keep their CANCELED status (preserves user cancellation intent).
   *  When false (default), CANCELED subscriptions are reactivated to ACTIVE.
   *  RENEWAL_FAILED and EXPIRED are always reactivated to ACTIVE regardless of this flag. */
  preserveStatus?: boolean;
}

/**
 * Creates a new subscription or extends/reactivates an existing one.
 *
 * Resolution priority:
 * 1. ACTIVE subscription with endDate > now
 * 2. CANCELED subscription with endDate > now
 * 3. RENEWAL_FAILED subscription (any endDate)
 * 4. EXPIRED subscription (most recent)
 * 5. No subscription found -> create new
 *
 * Behavior:
 * - Extend in place: stacks duration on top of existing endDate (unless grace period, then from now)
 * - Does NOT mutate planId, billingType, telegramChargeId, or renewal linkage on existing subs
 * - Expires any RENEWAL_FAILED subscriptions when creating a new subscription
 * - Supports test mode (1-minute duration)
 */
export async function createOrExtendSubscription(
  tx: TransactionClient,
  userId: string,
  durationDays: number,
  planId: string,
  billingType: BillingType,
  opts?: CreateOrExtendSubscriptionOptions,
): Promise<CreateOrExtendSubscriptionResult> {
  const now = new Date();
  const preserveStatus = opts?.preserveStatus ?? false;

  const testMode = await isSubscriptionTestMode();
  const durationMs = testMode
    ? 60 * 1000 // 1 minute in test mode
    : durationDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;

  // Find existing subscription to extend/reactivate
  // Priority: ACTIVE > CANCELED (with remaining time) > RENEWAL_FAILED > EXPIRED
  const existingSubscription =
    (await tx.subscription.findFirst({
      where: { userId, status: SubscriptionStatus.ACTIVE, endDate: { gt: now } },
      orderBy: { endDate: 'desc' },
    })) ??
    (await tx.subscription.findFirst({
      where: { userId, status: SubscriptionStatus.CANCELED, endDate: { gt: now } },
      orderBy: { endDate: 'desc' },
    })) ??
    (await tx.subscription.findFirst({
      where: { userId, status: SubscriptionStatus.RENEWAL_FAILED },
      orderBy: { endDate: 'desc' },
    })) ??
    (await tx.subscription.findFirst({
      where: { userId, status: SubscriptionStatus.EXPIRED },
      orderBy: { endDate: 'desc' },
    }));

  if (existingSubscription) {
    const previousStatus = existingSubscription.status;
    const previousEndDate = existingSubscription.endDate;

    // Determine the base date for extension:
    // - If endDate is in the future, stack from endDate
    // - If endDate is in the past (grace period / expired), extend from now
    const baseDate = existingSubscription.endDate > now
      ? existingSubscription.endDate
      : now;
    const newEndDate = new Date(baseDate.getTime() + durationMs);

    // Determine the new status
    let newStatus: SubscriptionStatus;
    if (previousStatus === SubscriptionStatus.CANCELED && preserveStatus) {
      // Preserve user cancellation intent
      newStatus = SubscriptionStatus.CANCELED;
    } else {
      // ACTIVE stays ACTIVE, CANCELED -> ACTIVE, RENEWAL_FAILED -> ACTIVE, EXPIRED -> ACTIVE
      newStatus = SubscriptionStatus.ACTIVE;
    }

    await tx.subscription.update({
      where: { id: existingSubscription.id },
      data: {
        endDate: newEndDate,
        status: newStatus,
      },
    });

    // Determine operation type
    let operation: CreateOrExtendSubscriptionResult['operation'];
    if (previousStatus === SubscriptionStatus.ACTIVE) {
      operation = 'extended';
    } else if (
      previousStatus === SubscriptionStatus.CANCELED &&
      newStatus === SubscriptionStatus.ACTIVE
    ) {
      operation = 'reactivated';
    } else if (
      previousStatus === SubscriptionStatus.RENEWAL_FAILED ||
      previousStatus === SubscriptionStatus.EXPIRED
    ) {
      operation = 'reactivated';
    } else {
      // CANCELED staying CANCELED is still an extension
      operation = 'extended';
    }

    logger.info('Extended/reactivated subscription', {
      subscriptionId: existingSubscription.id,
      userId,
      operation,
      previousStatus,
      newStatus,
      previousEndDate,
      newEndDate,
      testMode,
    });

    return {
      subscriptionId: existingSubscription.id,
      previousEndDate,
      newEndDate,
      operation,
      previousStatus,
      newStatus,
      expiredRenewalFailedSubs: [],
    };
  }

  // No existing subscription — create new
  // First, expire any RENEWAL_FAILED subscriptions to avoid ambiguity
  const renewalFailedSubs = await tx.subscription.findMany({
    where: { userId, status: SubscriptionStatus.RENEWAL_FAILED },
    select: { id: true, billingType: true },
  });

  if (renewalFailedSubs.length > 0) {
    await tx.subscription.updateMany({
      where: { userId, status: SubscriptionStatus.RENEWAL_FAILED },
      data: { status: SubscriptionStatus.EXPIRED },
    });
    logger.info('Expired RENEWAL_FAILED subscriptions before new subscription creation', {
      userId,
      expiredIds: renewalFailedSubs.map((s) => s.id),
    });
  }

  const endDate = new Date(now.getTime() + durationMs);

  const subscription = await tx.subscription.create({
    data: {
      userId,
      planId,
      status: SubscriptionStatus.ACTIVE,
      billingType,
      startDate: now,
      endDate,
      telegramChargeId: null,
    },
  });

  logger.info('Created new subscription', {
    subscriptionId: subscription.id,
    userId,
    planId,
    endDate,
    testMode,
  });

  return {
    subscriptionId: subscription.id,
    previousEndDate: null,
    newEndDate: endDate,
    operation: 'created',
    previousStatus: null,
    newStatus: SubscriptionStatus.ACTIVE,
    expiredRenewalFailedSubs: renewalFailedSubs,
  };
}

import redis from '../redis';
import logger from '../../logger/logger.server';
import { prisma } from '../prisma';
import { SubscriptionStatus } from '@generated/prisma/client';
import type { SubscriptionCacheData } from '@repo/shared';
import type { SubscriptionPlansCacheData } from '../redis/domains/subscription/types';
import { RECURRING_GRACE_HOURS } from '../monetization/constants';
import {
  resolveEffectiveStatus,
  toSubscriptionCacheData,
  interpretCachedStatus,
  type EffectiveSubscriptionStatus,
} from '../monetization/resolveEffectiveStatus';

/** TTL for negative cache entries (EXPIRED / NONE) — 5 minutes.
 * Admin deletion and payment webhooks explicitly invalidate Redis,
 * so this TTL is just a safety net, not the primary freshness mechanism. */
const NEGATIVE_CACHE_TTL = 60 * 5;

/**
 * Gets user's subscription status with optional caching
 * @param userId - The ID of the user
 * @param useCache - Whether to use cache (default: true)
 * @returns Subscription status data with active/expired/none status
 */
export async function getUserSubscriptionStatus(
  userId: string,
  useCache = true,
): Promise<{ id: string | null; status: EffectiveSubscriptionStatus; endDate: string | null; planName: string | null; planType: string; planId: string | null; billingType: string | null }> {
  // Try to get from cache first if enabled
  if (useCache) {
    const cachedSubscription = await redis.subscription.get(userId);
    if (cachedSubscription) {
      return interpretCachedStatus(cachedSubscription);
    }
  }

  // Single query to fetch all subscriptions for this user
  const allSubscriptions = await prisma.subscription.findMany({
    where: { userId },
    include: { plan: { select: { name: true, durationDays: true, planType: true } } },
    orderBy: { endDate: 'desc' },
  });

  if (allSubscriptions.length === 0) {
    // No subscription found — cache NONE sentinel to avoid repeated DB lookups
    if (useCache) {
      const noneSentinel: SubscriptionCacheData = {
        id: '',
        status: 'NONE',
        endDate: '',
        planName: '',
        durationDays: 0,
        planType: 'STANDARD',
        planId: '',
        billingType: null,
      };
      await redis.subscription.set(userId, noneSentinel, { ttl: NEGATIVE_CACHE_TTL });
    }

    return {
      id: null,
      status: 'NONE',
      endDate: null,
      planName: null,
      planType: 'STANDARD',
      planId: null,
      billingType: null,
    };
  }

  const resolved = resolveEffectiveStatus(allSubscriptions);

  // resolveEffectiveStatus returns null only for empty arrays, which we already handled
  const { subscription, effectiveStatus } = resolved!;
  const cacheData = toSubscriptionCacheData(subscription, effectiveStatus);

  if (useCache) {
    let ttl: number;

    if (effectiveStatus === SubscriptionStatus.ACTIVE) {
      const endDateMs = subscription.endDate.getTime();
      const nowMs = Date.now();
      const isInGraceWindow = endDateMs < nowMs;

      if (isInGraceWindow) {
        // RECURRING sub in 24h grace window — cap TTL at remaining grace time
        const graceEndMs = endDateMs + RECURRING_GRACE_HOURS * 60 * 60 * 1000;
        ttl = Math.max(60, Math.floor((graceEndMs - nowMs) / 1000));
      } else {
        const secondsUntilExpiry = Math.floor((endDateMs - nowMs) / 1000);
        const MAX_TTL = 60 * 60 * 24; // 24 hours
        const MIN_TTL = 60; // 1 minute floor
        ttl = Math.max(MIN_TTL, Math.min(MAX_TTL, secondsUntilExpiry));
      }
    } else {
      // CANCELED, RENEWAL_FAILED, EXPIRED — use negative cache TTL
      ttl = NEGATIVE_CACHE_TTL;
    }

    await redis.subscription.set(userId, cacheData, { ttl });
  }

  return {
    id: cacheData.id,
    status: cacheData.status,
    endDate: cacheData.endDate,
    planName: cacheData.planName,
    planType: cacheData.planType,
    planId: cacheData.planId,
    billingType: cacheData.billingType,
  };
}

/**
 * Phase 2 Optimization: Batched subscription and plans data retrieval using Redis pipeline
 * This reduces 2 separate Redis calls to 1 pipelined network request
 */
export async function getBatchedSubscriptionAndPlans(userId: string, botId: string): Promise<{
  subscription: SubscriptionCacheData | null;
  plans: SubscriptionPlansCacheData | null;
}> {
  try {
    logger.debug('Getting batched subscription and plans data via pipeline:', { userId });

    const batchResult = await redis.subscription.getBatched(userId, botId);

    const subscriptionData = batchResult.subscription
      ? batchResult.subscription as SubscriptionCacheData
      : null;

    const plansData = batchResult.plans
      ? batchResult.plans as SubscriptionPlansCacheData
      : null;

    return { subscription: subscriptionData, plans: plansData };
  } catch (error) {
    logger.error('Error in batched subscription and plans retrieval:', {
      error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
      userId,
    });
    return { subscription: null, plans: null };
  }
}
